name: ade-dedrm
description: Remove Adobe Digital Editions DRM from purchased EPUB/PDF books and optionally upload them to Calibre Web.
long_description:
Fulfills Adobe ACSM tickets, strips Adept DRM from EPUB/PDF files, and optionally uploads the decrypted result to a self-hosted Calibre Web instance. Designed to be driven entirely through natural-language tool calls from an MCP host such as Claude Desktop, so non-developer users never need to touch a terminal.

This is a personal-use tool. You are responsible for only using it on books you have legitimately purchased and are backing up for your own accessibility needs.

tools.name: status
tools.description: Check whether ade-dedrm state and Calibre Web settings are ready. Returns only booleans and the Calibre hostname (never credentials).

tools.name: bootstrap_ade
tools.description: Import Adobe Digital Editions activation state from the local install. macOS only. Triggers a macOS keychain prompt on first call; warn the user before invoking.

tools.name: decrypt
tools.description: Fulfill an .acsm ticket and/or strip Adept DRM from an .epub or .pdf file. Auto-detects input type. Requires bootstrap_ade() to have succeeded.

tools.name: upload_calibre
tools.description: Upload an already-decrypted .epub or .pdf to the configured Calibre Web instance. Requires Calibre URL/username/password to be set in user_config.

calibre_url.title: Calibre Web URL
calibre_url.description: Base URL of your self-hosted Calibre Web instance (e.g. https://calibre.example.com). Leave blank if you do not use Calibre Web.

calibre_username.title: Calibre Web Username
calibre_username.description: The username that has the 'upload' role on your Calibre Web instance.

calibre_password.title: Calibre Web Password
calibre_password.description: Stored securely by the host application and passed to the server via an environment variable. Never shown to the AI assistant.

calibre_verify_tls.title: Verify Calibre Web TLS Certificate
calibre_verify_tls.description: Turn off only if your Calibre Web uses a self-signed certificate and you understand the risk.
